name: filesize-block-then-comply
doc: SOLID file-size gate (core scope, evaluate() at src/policy/evaluate.ts:47-70). A Write whose content exceeds FUSE_SOLID_MAX_LINES (default 100, config/limits.ts:5) is denied with a `block` prompt rendered `[BLOCKED] SOLID file-size limit` (prompt/types.ts:24-27 formatPrompt tag). A subsequent small Edit of the same path takes the trivial fast path (gate-apex.ts:37, isTrivialEdit trivial-edits.ts:49-52, budget TRIVIAL_BUDGET=4 gate.ts:32) and is allowed. NOTE: this is CORE scope, not `solid` scope — the `solid` scope routes to validateSolidGate (Go/Python interface-location only, validate-solid.ts) and NEVER runs the file-size ceiling (that lives in core via evaluate()).

steps.doc: 150-line Write > 100 → file-size block. evaluate() runs file-size BEFORE the APEX gates (gate.ts runGates order line 58), so the deny is the size ceiling regardless of freshness. Deny shape: adapters/claude.ts denyResponse:30-34.

Same event again, asserting the human-readable tag + title are present in permissionDecisionReason (formatPrompt: `[BLOCKED] SOLID file-size limit`). PreToolUse is idempotent for a pure deny (no state write on a block beyond the deny-loop counter, which only rewrites the message on repeat — here the SAME payload so it would become [REPEAT]; we assert on the stable substring `SOLID file-size limit` which survives the [REPEAT] rewrite, deny-loop.ts:70 keeps the title suffix).

Compliant small Edit of the SAME path → allow (empty stdout). Proven by test/deny-loop.test.ts:92-97 (a small react Edit of a.ts passes the full gate via the trivial fast path). Different tool+input than the Writes above → distinct denyHash, so the anti-loop never touches this allowed call (deny-loop-store.ts:80).
